summary: Small documentation cleanup removing the obsolete “Building with Make (legacy)” section from .github/README.md. The README no longer points users at LEGACY_BUILD.md between the Containers and Diffing sections, reducing exposure to a legacy build path.
what_changed: Deleted the README section header `# Building with Make (legacy)`.
Deleted the link text `See [Building with Make](LEGACY_BUILD.md).` from `.github/README.md`.
No code, build scripts, headers, symbols, or matching-sensitive source files were changed.
smart_moves: Kept the change minimal and targeted: only removed the stale Make-based build pointer rather than rewriting unrelated README sections.
Reduced onboarding ambiguity by avoiding prominent documentation for a legacy build workflow.
decomp_lessons: Documentation should steer contributors toward the currently supported build workflow; stale legacy build instructions can waste setup/debugging time.
For decomp projects with evolving tooling, remove obsolete setup paths from high-visibility docs once they are no longer recommended.
Small docs-only PRs are useful for keeping contributor guidance aligned with current infrastructure, even when they do not affect matching.
review_feedback: Only visible human response was a brief approval/thanks comment: `Thanks!`
No review comments or detailed reviewer feedback were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Check whether LEGACY_BUILD.md still exists and whether other docs still link to it.
Search repository documentation for remaining references to `Building with Make`, `LEGACY_BUILD.md`, or legacy Make workflows.
